commands.name: openfang-config
commands.description: Inspect OpenFang configuration through the installed CLI.
commands.notes: Exact subcommands may vary; run openfang --help if this CLI differs.

commands.name: openfang-doctor
commands.description: Run OpenFang health checks for local agent runtime configuration.
commands.notes: Use after broad OpenFang skill, config, or runtime edits.

commands.name: zeroclaw-config-check
commands.description: Validate ZeroClaw config TOML with Python's tomllib on Python versions that support module execution.
commands.notes: Requires Python 3.11 or newer for tomllib.

commands.name: zeroclaw-config-edit
commands.description: Edit ZeroClaw's local TOML configuration.
commands.notes: Validate TOML syntax after editing because startup failures can come from malformed config.

commands.name: astro-build
commands.description: Build an Astro site using the project's Bun script.
commands.notes: Run from the Astro project root.

commands.name: astro-check
commands.description: Run Astro diagnostics and TypeScript checks.
commands.notes: Pair with the repo's build command before publishing.

commands.name: astro-create
commands.description: Create an Astro project for content-heavy sites or landing pages.
commands.notes: Use Astro when the project is content or landing-page oriented.

commands.name: biome-check
commands.description: Run Biome formatting and lint checks across the current project.
commands.notes: Use --write only when you intend to apply safe fixes.

commands.name: biome-init
commands.description: Create a Biome configuration file in a JS/TS project.
commands.notes: Review generated rules before committing.

commands.name: biome-write
commands.description: Apply Biome formatting and safe lint fixes.
commands.notes: Review the diff after running because this can touch many files.

commands.name: bun-add-dev-biome
commands.description: Install Biome as the default formatter and linter for JS/TS projects.
commands.notes: Prefer Biome by default unless the repo already has a stronger formatter/linter convention.

commands.name: docker-compose-config
commands.description: Render the fully resolved Docker Compose configuration.
commands.notes: Useful for catching environment interpolation and YAML merge issues.

commands.name: docker-compose-down-volumes
commands.description: Stop a Compose stack and remove project volumes plus orphaned containers.
commands.notes: This deletes local database volume data; use when a full reset is intended.

commands.name: docker-compose-health
commands.description: Show Compose service state, health, and published ports in a compact table.
commands.notes: Health is blank for services without a healthcheck.

commands.name: docker-compose-logs
commands.description: Follow recent logs for one Docker Compose service.
commands.notes: Omit SERVICE to follow all services in the Compose project.

commands.name: docker-compose-ps
commands.description: Show container status, health, ports, and service names for a Compose project.
commands.notes: Run from the directory containing compose.yaml.

commands.name: docker-compose-up-build
commands.description: Build and start all services in a Docker Compose project.
commands.notes: Default local services workflow for Tim's projects.

commands.name: docker-container-shell
commands.description: Open an interactive shell inside a running Compose service container.
commands.notes: Use bash instead of sh when the image includes it.

commands.name: docker-disk-usage
commands.description: Show Docker disk usage by images, containers, volumes, and build cache.
commands.notes: Review before pruning images or volumes.

commands.name: json-extract-field
commands.description: Extract one field from each object in a JSON array.
commands.notes: Adjust the path to match the file shape.

commands.name: json-pretty
commands.description: Pretty-print and validate a JSON file.
commands.notes: jq exits nonzero when the JSON is invalid.

commands.name: drizzle-generate
commands.description: Generate Drizzle SQL migrations from TypeScript schema changes.
commands.notes: Use Drizzle for TypeScript database access in Next.js/BFF projects.

commands.name: drizzle-migrate
commands.description: Apply generated Drizzle migrations to the configured database.
commands.notes: Check drizzle.config before running against shared databases.

commands.name: drizzle-studio
commands.description: Open Drizzle Studio for inspecting and editing local database data.
commands.notes: Use on local or disposable branch databases unless edits are intentional.

commands.name: disk-usage-depth1
commands.description: Show disk usage of immediate children under the current directory, sorted by size.
commands.notes: Run from the directory you want to inspect.

commands.name: find-large-files
commands.description: Find files larger than 500 MB below the current directory.
commands.notes: Adjust +500M to the threshold you care about.

commands.name: find-recent-files
commands.description: Find files modified in the last 24 hours.
commands.notes: Use -mmin -30 for files changed in the last 30 minutes.

commands.name: permissions-octal
commands.description: Print octal permissions and owner/group for a file or directory.
commands.notes: Useful before changing chmod/chown settings.

commands.name: rsync-dry-run
commands.description: Preview a recursive sync before copying files.
commands.notes: Remove --dry-run only after reviewing the planned changes.

commands.name: tar-zstd-create
commands.description: Create a zstd-compressed tar archive.
commands.notes: Use tar --zstd -tf archive.tar.zst to list archive contents.

commands.name: git-branch-cleanup-merged
commands.description: List local branches already merged into main.
commands.notes: Review before deleting branches.

commands.name: git-log-graph
commands.description: Show recent branch history as a compact decorated graph.
commands.notes: Increase -30 when you need more history.

commands.name: git-restore-staged
commands.description: Unstage a file without discarding its working-tree changes.
commands.notes: Replace PATH with the file or directory to unstage.

commands.name: git-show-staged
commands.description: Review staged changes before committing.
commands.notes: Use git diff for unstaged changes.

commands.name: git-status-porcelain
commands.description: Show concise working-tree status suitable for quick review.
commands.notes: Use before staging so unrelated changes stay visible.

commands.name: bun-install-frozen
commands.description: Install dependencies exactly as recorded in bun.lock.
commands.notes: Use in CI or when you do not want lockfile changes.

commands.name: bun-run-check
commands.description: Run a project-defined check script through Bun.
commands.notes: Actual behavior depends on package.json scripts.

commands.name: mcp-inspect-node
commands.description: Inspect an MCP server command with the official MCP inspector.
commands.notes: Replace COMMAND with the full server command and arguments.

commands.name: mcp-server-python
commands.description: Run a Python MCP server module directly for local handshake/debug work.
commands.notes: Replace package.mcp_server with the actual module path.

commands.name: dns-debug
commands.description: Ask systemd-resolved to resolve a hostname and show the resolver path.
commands.notes: Use resolvectl status to inspect configured DNS servers.

commands.name: http-headers
commands.description: Fetch response headers without downloading the body.
commands.notes: Add -L to follow redirects.

commands.name: http-timing
commands.cmd: curl -o /dev/null -s -w 'dns=%{time_namelookup} connect=%{time_connect} tls=%{time_appconnect} start=%{time_starttransfer} total=%{time_total}\n' https://example.com
commands.description: Measure DNS, TCP, TLS, first-byte, and total request time for a URL.
commands.notes: Good first check before deeper browser or server profiling.

commands.name: ip-addresses
commands.description: Show network interfaces and assigned addresses in a compact table.
commands.notes: Use ip route for default route and routing table.

commands.name: listening-ports
commands.description: Show TCP ports listening on the machine with owning processes.
commands.notes: Use ss -lunp for UDP listeners.

commands.name: process-on-port
commands.description: Find the process listening on one TCP port.
commands.notes: Replace PORT with the numeric port, for example 3000.

commands.name: bun-create-next
commands.description: Scaffold a TypeScript Next.js App Router project using Bun.
commands.notes: Select project options deliberately; keep App Router as the default for new web apps.

commands.name: next-build
commands.description: Build a Next.js app for production.
commands.notes: Catches route, server/client boundary, and type integration issues that lint may miss.

commands.name: next-dev
commands.description: Start the Next.js development server through the project's Bun script.
commands.notes: Use the repo's package.json scripts as source of truth.

commands.name: nix-build-package
commands.description: Build one package output from the current flake.
commands.notes: Use nix flake show to find the package name.

commands.name: nix-develop
commands.description: Enter the default development shell for a flake-based project.
commands.notes: Use Nix shells as the default way to get project toolchains and system libraries.

commands.name: nix-develop-command
commands.description: Run a command inside a flake dev shell without staying in the shell.
commands.notes: Good for CI-like local checks where dependencies must come from the flake.

commands.name: nix-flake-check
commands.description: Evaluate and run all checks, packages, dev shells, and NixOS modules exposed by the current flake.
commands.notes: Run in the flake repository root.

commands.name: nix-flake-show
commands.description: Show the packages, apps, checks, dev shells, and NixOS modules exposed by a flake.
commands.notes: Run before guessing package or app names.

commands.name: nix-flake-update-input
commands.description: Update one flake input while leaving other locked inputs unchanged.
commands.notes: Replace nixpkgs with the input name you want to update.

commands.name: nix-gc-old-generations
commands.description: Delete system and user generations older than 14 days, then garbage collect unused store paths.
commands.notes: Adjust the age window if you need a longer rollback history.

commands.name: nix-run-app
commands.description: Run a flake app output and pass arguments to the app.
commands.notes: The double dash separates nix options from app options.

commands.name: nix-shell-package
commands.description: Open a temporary shell with selected packages available on PATH.
commands.notes: Replace package names with the tools you need.

commands.name: nix-store-optimize
commands.description: Hard-link duplicate files inside the Nix store to reduce disk usage.
commands.notes: Safe but can take time on large stores.

commands.name: nix-store-roots
commands.description: List garbage-collection roots that keep store paths alive.
commands.notes: Use before deleting profiles or wondering why disk usage stayed high.

commands.name: nix-why-depends
commands.description: Explain why the current system closure depends on a package path.
commands.notes: Replace openssl with the package you are investigating.

commands.name: nixos-build-vm
commands.description: Build a local VM from the selected NixOS host configuration.
commands.notes: Useful before switching major system modules.

commands.name: nixos-generation-list
commands.description: List system generations available for rollback.
commands.notes: Use before garbage collection if you need to preserve rollback points.

commands.name: nixos-option
commands.description: Inspect the effective value and declaration location for a NixOS option.
commands.notes: Replace the option path with the module option you are investigating.

commands.name: nixos-repl-flake
commands.description: Open a Nix REPL with the current flake loaded.
commands.notes: Useful for inspecting outputs and module values interactively.

commands.name: nixos-switch
commands.description: Build and activate the NixOS system configuration for the current host.
commands.notes: Run from anywhere if the flake path is correct. Replace the host selector when building another machine.

commands.name: nixos-test
commands.description: Build and activate a NixOS configuration until the next reboot without making it the boot default.
commands.notes: Good for risky service, kernel, display, and networking changes.

commands.name: opencode-help
commands.description: Show OpenCode CLI options for the installed version.
commands.notes: Check the local CLI instead of assuming flags.

commands.name: opencode-run
commands.description: Start OpenCode in the current repository.
commands.notes: Use OpenCode as the default coding harness when working interactively.

commands.name: neon-connection-test
commands.description: Verify a Postgres or Neon DATABASE_URL connects and returns basic identity info.
commands.notes: Confirms the URL, credentials, SSL handling, and network path.

commands.name: postgres-active-queries
commands.cmd: psql "$DATABASE_URL" -c "select pid, state, wait_event_type, now() - query_start as age, left(query, 120) as query from pg_stat_activity where state <> 'idle' order by query_start;"
commands.description: List currently active Postgres queries with wait state and age.
commands.notes: Useful when an app appears hung or slow.

commands.name: postgres-connect
commands.description: Open psql using the DATABASE_URL environment variable.
commands.notes: Make sure DATABASE_URL is set in the current shell.

commands.name: postgres-extensions
commands.cmd: psql "$DATABASE_URL" -c 'select extname, extversion from pg_extension order by extname;'
commands.description: List installed Postgres extensions and versions.
commands.notes: Useful before relying on uuid, vector, trigram, or crypto features.

commands.name: postgres-locks
commands.cmd: psql "$DATABASE_URL" -c "select locktype, relation::regclass, mode, granted, pid from pg_locks where not granted order by pid;"
commands.description: Show ungranted Postgres locks that may be blocking migrations or requests.
commands.notes: Pair with pg_stat_activity when debugging stuck queries.

commands.name: postgres-table-sizes
commands.cmd: psql "$DATABASE_URL" -c "select relname, pg_size_pretty(pg_total_relation_size(relid)) as size from pg_catalog.pg_statio_user_tables order by pg_total_relation_size(relid) desc;"
commands.description: Show user tables ordered by total on-disk size.
commands.notes: Includes indexes and TOAST storage.

commands.name: cpu-memory-top
commands.description: Show the top CPU-consuming processes with parent PID and command.
commands.notes: Change --sort=-%mem to rank by memory.

commands.name: kill-tree-preview
commands.description: Inspect a process tree before stopping a parent process.
commands.notes: Preview first so you know which child processes will be affected.

commands.name: watch-process
commands.description: Refresh one process status every second.
commands.notes: Replace PID with the process id.

commands.name: python-module-run
commands.description: Run a Python module using import-safe module execution.
commands.notes: Prefer this over running files directly when package imports matter.

commands.name: python-mypy
commands.description: Run mypy type checking for typed Python modules.
commands.notes: Best for small typed modules and MCP/data tooling where explicit contracts matter.

commands.name: python-pytest
commands.description: Run Python tests quietly through the active interpreter.
commands.notes: Using python -m keeps the test runner tied to the selected environment.

commands.name: python-ruff-check
commands.description: Run Ruff lint checks for Python projects that use Ruff.
commands.notes: Only use where Ruff is already part of the repo or explicitly chosen.

commands.name: python-venv
commands.description: Create and activate a local Python virtual environment for scripts or MCP tooling.
commands.notes: Use Python for glue code, data transforms, and MCP support unless the repo convention says otherwise.

commands.name: cargo-add-axum-stack
commands.cmd: cargo add axum tokio tower tower-http tracing tracing-subscriber serde serde_json anyhow thiserror
commands.description: Add the common Rust web API dependency set.
commands.notes: Enable tokio features as needed in Cargo.toml, commonly rt-multi-thread and macros.

commands.name: cargo-add-sqlx-postgres
commands.description: Add SQLx with the Postgres feature set normally used for Tim's Rust services.
commands.notes: Use SQLx for Rust database access instead of introducing a different ORM.

commands.name: cargo-check-workspace
commands.description: Type-check all workspace crates, examples, tests, and binaries.
commands.notes: Faster than full tests when iterating on compile errors.

commands.name: cargo-clippy-strict
commands.description: Run Clippy across the workspace and fail on warnings.
commands.notes: Good before commits on Rust backend changes.

commands.name: cargo-fmt-check
commands.description: Check Rust formatting without rewriting files.
commands.notes: Run cargo fmt --all to apply formatting.

commands.name: cargo-new-api
commands.description: Create a new Rust binary crate suitable for an Axum API service.
commands.notes: Use this as the starting point for durable backend services before adding axum, tokio, tracing, sqlx, and config crates.

commands.name: cargo-nextest
commands.description: Run Rust workspace tests with nextest when the project provides it.
commands.notes: Use cargo test if nextest is not installed or not configured.

commands.name: cargo-run-tracing
commands.description: Run a Rust service with useful tracing output for request and middleware debugging.
commands.notes: Tune module filters when a specific crate is too noisy.

commands.name: cargo-test-one
commands.description: Run Rust tests matching a name and show stdout/stderr.
commands.notes: Replace TEST_NAME with a substring of the test path.

commands.name: ripgrep-context
commands.description: Search for a pattern with line numbers and two context lines.
commands.notes: Omit PATH to search the current directory.

commands.name: ripgrep-files
commands.description: List files under the current directory while respecting ignore files.
commands.notes: Faster and cleaner than find for most source trees.

commands.name: ripgrep-hidden
commands.description: Search hidden files while excluding common large generated directories.
commands.notes: Useful for dotfiles and config repositories.

commands.name: chmod-fix-private-key
commands.description: Set private SSH key permissions to the mode OpenSSH expects.
commands.notes: Only use on private key files, not public .pub files.

commands.name: gpg-list-secret-keys
commands.description: List local GPG secret keys with long key IDs.
commands.notes: Useful before configuring git commit signing or agenix identities.

commands.name: ssh-verbose
commands.description: Run SSH with verbose negotiation logs for authentication and config debugging.
commands.notes: Avoid sharing full output publicly because it can reveal paths and host details.

commands.name: tls-cert-check
commands.cmd: openssl s_client -connect example.com:443 -servername example.com </dev/null 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -subject -issuer -dates
commands.description: Inspect the served TLS certificate subject, issuer, and validity dates.
commands.notes: Replace both example.com values with the target host.

commands.name: bash-strict-template
commands.description: Print the standard portable Bash script header.
commands.notes: Use Bash for portable scripts rather than zsh-specific features.

commands.name: shellcheck-file
commands.description: Lint a shell script with ShellCheck.
commands.notes: Run when editing non-trivial Bash or POSIX shell scripts.

commands.name: zsh-reload
commands.description: Reload the interactive zsh login shell after editing shell configuration.
commands.notes: Use zsh for interactive workflows on this machine.

commands.name: sqlx-create-db
commands.description: Create the Postgres database referenced by DATABASE_URL.
commands.notes: Requires DATABASE_URL to be set.

commands.name: sqlx-migrate-add
commands.description: Create a new SQLx migration file.
commands.notes: Use a descriptive snake_case migration name.

commands.name: sqlx-migrate-revert
commands.description: Revert the most recent SQLx migration when the migration has a down step.
commands.notes: Use only on disposable local or branch databases unless rollback has been reviewed.

commands.name: sqlx-migrate-run
commands.description: Apply pending SQLx migrations to the database in DATABASE_URL.
commands.notes: Run against the intended local or branch database.

commands.name: sqlx-prepare
commands.description: Refresh SQLx offline query metadata for the whole Rust workspace.
commands.notes: Commit the generated .sqlx metadata when the repo uses offline query checking.

commands.name: journal-follow-unit
commands.description: Follow the latest logs for a system service.
commands.notes: Use --user for user units and replace UNIT.service with the real unit name.

commands.name: systemd-analyze-blame
commands.description: Show units ordered by startup time contribution.
commands.notes: Combine with systemd-analyze critical-chain for boot ordering.

commands.name: systemd-critical-chain
commands.description: Show the boot dependency chain that delayed reaching the default target.
commands.notes: Useful when boot feels slow but blame alone is misleading.

commands.name: systemd-failed
commands.description: List failed system units that need investigation.
commands.notes: Run systemctl reset-failed UNIT after fixing a unit if you want to clear old failure state.

commands.name: systemd-unit-status
commands.description: Show service state, recent logs, PID, exit code, and unit metadata.
commands.notes: Add --user for per-user services.

commands.name: next-typecheck
commands.description: Run TypeScript checking without writing output files.
commands.notes: Use when the repo does not already expose a check script.

commands.name: bun-add-shadcn
commands.description: Initialize shadcn/ui in a React or Next.js project.
commands.notes: Use shadcn/ui when possible for Tim's default UI stack.

commands.name: shadcn-add-components
commands.cmd: bunx shadcn@latest add button card dialog dropdown-menu input label select tabs textarea toast
commands.description: Add a practical baseline set of shadcn/ui components.
commands.notes: Trim the list to the components actually needed for the feature.

commands.name: tailwind-merge-classes
commands.description: Install common class composition helpers used by shadcn-style React UI.
commands.notes: Usually already present after shadcn init; use when wiring a project manually.

commands.name: nuxt-create
commands.description: Create a Nuxt app when the project is already Vue/Nuxt oriented.
commands.notes: Use Nuxt as a frontend alternative only when it fits the repo direction.

commands.name: vue-create
commands.description: Create a Vue project when the repo or product direction is already Vue.
commands.notes: Prefer Next.js for new default web apps unless Vue is the existing convention.

commands.name: html-serve-static
commands.description: Serve static HTML/CSS files from the current directory.
commands.notes: Useful for quick web primitive checks without adding a framework.

commands.name: playwright-screenshot
commands.description: Capture a browser screenshot of a local web app for visual verification.
commands.notes: Start the dev server first and adjust the URL/port.
